perturbation.original_text: The schedule set forth in Exhibit A will constitute invoicing for the events. Notwithstanding the above, Torvec may, in its sole discretion, cancel its participation in any of the above-referenced events by notifying Vendor of such cancellation not later than two weeks prior to the due date(s) for payment hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: The schedule set forth in Exhibit A will constitute invoicing for the events. Notwithstanding the above, Torvec may, in its sole discretion, cancel its participation in any of the above-referenced events by notifying Vendor of such cancellation not later than one week prior to the due date(s) for payment hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'two weeks' to 'one week' creates an in-text contradiction. This modifies the deadline for cancellation notification which can be problematic since one area of the contract states two weeks, while another states one week. This can lead to legal dispute due to uncleared terms. This contradiction directly impacts the payment terms and cancellation rights outlined in Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment and Other Consideration As consideration for the benefits it receives under this Agreement, Torvec shall provide the following to Vendor: See Exhibit A The schedule set forth in Exhibit A will constitute invoicing for the events. Notwithstanding the above, Torvec may, in its sole discretion, cancel its participation in any of the above-referenced events by notifying Vendor of such cancellation not later than one week prior to the due date(s) for payment hereunder. All checks shall be made payabe to: Phoenix Performance, LLC and mailed to 481, Schuylkill Rd, Phoenixville, Pa. 19460. (b) Equipment
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the cancellation notification period from 'two weeks' to 'one week'. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, 'not later than one week prior to the due date(s) for payment hereunder.' However, the explanation indicates that another area of the contract states 'two weeks'. This creates a direct inconsistency regarding the cancellation deadline, which falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, as different sections of the contract provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: During the Term of this Agreement, Torvec shall be entitled to the following sponsorship benefits: a) Primary sponsorship rights to all of Vendor's participation efforts in the above race events. b) The right to specify and approve all team sponsorship identification markings, logos, graphics, etc. for vehicle livery, team equipment and uniforms. c) Sole right to disapprove any driver selected by Vendor for any reason. The driver for these events will be John Heinricy.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term of this Agreement, Torvec shall be entitled to the following sponsorship benefits: a) Primary sponsorship rights to all of Vendor's participation efforts in the above race events. b) The right to specify and approve all team sponsorship identification markings, logos, graphics, etc. for vehicle livery, team equipment and uniforms. c) Sole right to disapprove any driver selected by Vendor for any reason. The driver for these events may be John Heinricy.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will be' to 'may be' introduces uncertainty regarding the driver selection. This contradicts the apparent guarantee that John Heinricy would be the driver, creating a contradiction in Section 4. Because of this, the sponsorship rights for Torvec might also be unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Torvec Benefits During the Term of this Agreement, Torvec shall be entitled to the following sponsorship benefits: a) Primary sponsorship rights to all of Vendor's participation efforts in the above race events. b) The right to specify and approve all team sponsorship identification markings, logos, graphics, etc. for vehicle livery, team equipment and uniforms. c) Sole right to disapprove any driver selected by Vendor for any reason. The driver for these events may be John Heinricy.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated "The driver for these events will be John Heinricy," creating a definitive statement. The changed text modifies this to "The driver for these events may be John Heinricy," introducing uncertainty and optionality regarding the driver. This creates a direct contradiction because the original text implies a guarantee, while the changed text removes that guarantee. This qualifies as an inconsistency leading to an in-text contradiction, as defined in the provided documentation.

perturbation.original_text: Vendor represents and warrants to Torvec the following: (i) it has the authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform hereunder in accordance with its provisions; (ii) no other person or entity has the right to be the exclusive automotive sponsor of the activities and the events set forth in this Agreement; and (iii) it will perform its obligations under this Agreement in compliance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor represents and warrants to Torvec the following: (i) it has the authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform hereunder in accordance with its provisions; (ii) other persons or entities might have the right to be the exclusive automotive sponsor of the activities and the events set forth in this Agreement; and (iii) it will perform its obligations under this Agreement in compliance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'no other person or entity has the right' to 'other persons or entities might have the right' directly contradicts the representation made by the Vendor. This change creates an in-text contradiction in Section 9. Because of this contradiction, legal disputes could arise. This now affects the entire agreement, potentially voiding it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Representations Vendor represents and warrants to Torvec the following: (i) it has the authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform hereunder in accordance with its provisions; (ii) other persons or entities might have the right to be the exclusive automotive sponsor of the activities and the events set forth in this Agreement; and (iii) it will perform its obligations under this Agreement in compliance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'no other person or entity has the right to be the exclusive automotive sponsor,' while the changed text indicates that 'other persons or entities might have the right.' This directly contradicts the original representation and creates a clear inconsistency within the agreement regarding the exclusivity of the automotive sponsorship. The Contradicted Text and Changed Text are in the same location to show this direct contradiction.
